title: Sprint-93 Closeout — Stage1 v1.5.0 Rollup Consumer Surfaces

closeoutRule.result: HELD — zero retro-splits on a repeat run; rule is now validated as standing convention

s93-m01.summary: extend structuredData_fetch with Stage1 v1.5.0 rollup kinds

s93-m02.summary: capability normalizer — fold rollups into first-class view

s93-m03.summary: real-data E2E gate vs v1.5.0 rollups (dc1cfabb + 07776e70)

s93-m04.summary: playground capability view — render normalized rollup output

s93Deliverables: structuredData.fetch kind+runPath mode accepts identity_graph (1.1.0), capability_rollup (1.1.0), object_rollup (1.0.0)
schema_version allow-list enforcement (fast-fail on unknown versions)
normalizeCapabilities() pure transform: capability spine from capability_rollup, evidence-ref join from object_rollup, identity_node attach from identity_graph
real-data gate against dc1cfabb + 07776e70 in test/e2e/stage1-rollups.e2e.spec.ts (6 tests, auto-skips when Stage1 absent)
playground CapabilityPanel rendering normalized output on static real-sample fixtures

deliverables: structuredData.fetch kind+runPath input mode (oneOf gated against dataset mode)
Stage1RollupKind + Stage1IdentityGraph + Stage1CapabilityRollup + Stage1ObjectRollup type exports
12 co-located handler tests + 6 new contract-schema tests (all pass)

evidence.determinismLinear: 7 capabilities / 7 presentations / 0 derived variants — byte-identical across repeat runs
evidence.determinismStripe: 12 capabilities / 15 presentations / 0 derived variants — byte-identical across repeat runs
evidence.joinSemantics: evidence-ref tuple equality (artifact_ref + json_pointer + run_id); 0 real-data overlaps reflect current Stage1 emission, not a normalizer bug

evidence.schemaVersionFastFail: MET — forged 9.9.9 rejected with structured error
evidence.etagStability: MET — identical etag + sizeBytes on repeat fetches
evidence.normalizerDeterminism: MET — byte-identical stringify across both fixtures

deliverables: STAGE1_FIXTURES extended from 4 → 6 entries (new kind="rollups" discriminator)

evidence.devServerProbe: rollups.ts / CapabilityPanel.tsx / linear-v15-rollups.json all serve HTTP 200
evidence.manualBrowserValidation: left to maintainer per mission text — dev server smoke confirms module graph resolves

gateResults.newMcpSurfaceSizeRationale: m02 intentionally shipped without a capability.resolve MCP tool surface — per mission scope 'No new MCP tool surface'. Playground consumes via internal transform; if a tool surface is needed it lands in a future sprint after consumer DX proves value.
gateResults.regressionProfile: All 2166+ existing tests remain green in isolation; pre-existing cross-suite isolation flakes (registry-snapshot.spec + stage1-integration.spec, documented in s92 closeout) are unrelated and pass individually.

carryForward: Cross-suite test-isolation flakes persist (mapping.spec vs map-apply.spec shared mappings file); housekeeping candidate for sprint-94
Rollup-write scope (OODS producing rollups) remains out of scope per sprint-93 charter; only consumer-side surfaces are active
Stage1 sprint-46 ConfidenceDecomposition may bump rollup schema_versions; m01's allow-list is explicit so any bump fails fast — follow-up mission or scope extension expected

Current Stage1 v1.5.0 emission yields 0 direct evidence-ref overlaps between capability member_instances and object_rollup projection_variants (action_candidates/* vs objects/*/clusters/*); a future Stage1 emitter change or a richer join strategy could light this up
Playground capability-normalizer mirror duplicates mcp-server source; a future sprint could collapse this into a shared @oods/stage1 package if consumer surface grows
